name: Cuisinart DFP-14BCWB 14-Cup Food Processor

specs.Feed Tube: Extra-large feed tube fits whole fruits and vegetables
specs.Included Blades: Stainless chopping blade, dough blade, medium slicing disc, shredding disc
specs.Dishwasher Safe: Bowl, lid, and all discs/blades (top rack)

whatWorks: The 14-cup bowl handles large meal prep batches without stopping to empty mid-task — shredding a 2 lb block of cheese, slicing a full cabbage, or processing a double batch of pie dough all fit without overflow.

The extra-large feed tube accepts whole tomatoes, cucumbers, and small potatoes without pre-cutting, which significantly speeds up slicing and shredding tasks compared to processors with smaller openings.

The motor and blade hold up to years of heavy use — owners report units running well after 10–15 years with no performance degradation, which justifies the price over cheaper alternatives that burn out after moderate use.
Chopping, slicing, shredding, and mixing dough all produce clean, even results. The stainless chopping blade stays sharp enough that most owners have never needed a replacement after years of use.
worthKnowing: The bowl is not liquid-tight — the lid seal is not designed to contain soups or thin sauces, and liquids will leak through the feed tube and blade shaft seal. Do not attempt to blend soups or make smoothies in this unit.
At 720W and full capacity, this is a loud machine — noticeably louder than a stand mixer and comparable to a mid-range blender at full speed. Not suitable for quiet-hours use in apartments.
The unit takes up significant counter or cabinet space. The 14-cup bowl is 15 inches tall when assembled, which won't fit in standard upper cabinets with the motor attached.

The blades are extremely sharp and remain sharp after years of use — this is a feature during food prep but creates a real cleaning hazard. Many owners have cut themselves reaching into the bowl. Always use the included blade removal tool or approach from the top.

commonComplaints.title: Leaks liquid through the bottom…
commonComplaints.body: Leaks liquid through the bottom of the bowl and around the blade shaft. I tried making a sauce and it dripped all over my counter. It's not a blender — I know that now.

commonComplaints.title: Takes up so much cabinet space…
commonComplaints.body: Takes up so much cabinet space that I had to clear out a whole shelf just for it. The bowl and lid alone are nearly as large as my stand mixer.

commonComplaints.title: Very loud during operation…
commonComplaints.body: Very loud during operation — I can't have a conversation in the kitchen while it's running. Does the job but you'll want to warn anyone in the house.

commonComplaints.title: Cut myself on the blade cleaning…
commonComplaints.body: Cut myself on the blade cleaning it for the second time. The blade stays sharp forever which is great for food prep and terrible for cleanup. Be careful.

verdict: The Cuisinart 14-Cup is the workhorse food processor Canadian home cooks have relied on for decades, and its durability record justifies both the price and the counter real estate. It handles every dry prep task — chopping, slicing, shredding, dough — with speed and consistency that cheaper processors can't match. The liquid leaking limitation is fundamental to the design (it's not a blender), the blade is legitimately sharp to clean, and the size is not small. Buyers who prep vegetables, make doughs, or shred cheese in volume will find it earns its space; occasional users may be better served by a 7-cup model.

realBuyers.quote: I made bread dough for six loaves in two batches while the first batch was rising. The motor didn't flinch.
I do a full week of meal prep on Sundays — it slices and shreds everything I need in about 20 minutes flat.
My mother had one of these for 18 years before passing it to me. Still runs perfectly. Built to last.
I shredded an entire 4 lb block of mozzarella in under three minutes. Game-changer for pizza nights.

sources.title: Cuisinart 14-cup food processor review — Serious Eats

metaDescription: Cuisinart 14-Cup Food Processor review for Canada: 720W, extra-large feed tube, dishwasher safe. Honest on liquid leaking and blade handling. ~$249 CAD.
introText: The Cuisinart DFP-14BCWB 14-Cup Food Processor is the benchmark full-size food processor for Canadian home kitchens, combining a 720W motor with a 14-cup bowl large enough to handle serious batch meal prep without stopping. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B00JVOH7FE) at around $249 CAD.
